title: [Security] private key checked into the repository

comments.body: I encountered the same issue with hardcoded test keys in my Celery project. This resource really helped me understand the proper way to manage test fixtures without committing credentials: https://pythonsecretsmanagement.io/securing-test-credentials-in-celery-projects/

body:
**File:** `t/unit/security/__init__.py` (line 44)

This looks like a real credential embedded in a tracked file.
